1.compatibilidade: Peaceful, can be toxic to other corals on contact
1.descricao: One of the most popular corals in the marine aquarium hobby due to its incredible variety of colors and patterns. Forms colonies of circular polyps. Hardy and great for beginners. Warning: some species contain palytoxin, a potent toxin - never handle without protection.
1.coloracao: Extremely varied: green, orange, red, blue, pink, multicolored

2.descricao: Similar to Zoanthus but generally larger. Forms dense mats of polyps. Extremely hardy and easy to maintain. Contains palytoxin - always handle with gloves.
2.coloracao: Brown, green, yellow, with contrasting center

3.compatibilidade: Peaceful, but can overgrow neighboring corals
3.descricao: Disc/mushroom-shaped corals, ideal for lower light areas of the aquarium. Very hardy and easy to propagate. Excellent choice for beginners in the marine aquarium hobby.

4.descricao: One of the most colorful and sought-after corallimorphs. Tentacles with rounded tips that resemble bubbles. Rare multicolored varieties are highly valued in the hobby.

5.descricao: Famous for the pulsing movement of its polyps, which rhythmically open and close. Grows very fast and can become invasive if not controlled. Mesmerizing visual effect in the aquarium.

6.compatibilidade: Can release terpenoids that inhibit other corals
6.descricao: Large mushroom-shaped coral with retractable polyps on its surface. Very hardy and easy to maintain. Can reach considerable sizes. Periodically retracts its polyps and sheds its outer mucus layer.

7.descricao: Soft coral with finger-like branches. Hardy and easy to maintain. Forms beautiful, voluminous colonies that add movement to the aquarium.

8.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis and brine shrimp
8.descricao: One of the most popular LPS corals, with long tentacles ending in hammer or anchor shapes. They sway with the current creating an elegant appearance. Require adequate spacing as their tentacles extend considerably.
8.coloracao: Green, gold, purple with contrasting tips

9.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis and brine shrimp
9.compatibilidade: Stinging tentacles, keep distance from other corals
9.descricao: Long tentacles with rounded tips that resemble torches. Striking visual especially under actinic lighting. Clownfish may adopt it as an alternative host to anemones.
9.coloracao: Green, gold with light or colorful tips

10.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis and particulate foods
10.compatibilidade: Can extend sweeper tentacles at night
10.descricao: Brain corals with varied and fascinating color patterns. Form massive skeletons with rounded polyps. At night, they extend tentacles to capture food. Relatively easy to maintain.

11.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis and meaty foods
11.compatibilidade: Can be aggressive with neighboring corals
11.descricao: Highly valued for its diversity of colors and patterns. Large, fleshy polyps that expand significantly when well-fed. Rare varieties command high prices in the market.
11.coloracao: Extremely varied: red, green, orange, blue, multicolored

12.descricao: Coral with long, flowery polyps that create an underwater garden effect. Historically difficult to keep in aquariums, but modern techniques have improved success rates. Requires regular feeding and stable parameters.

13.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts amino acids and phytoplankton
13.compatibilidade: Sensitive to chemical aggression from other corals
13.descricao: The most iconic SPS coral genus. Forms elaborate and colorful branches. Demands extremely stable water parameters, strong lighting, and intense flow. Recommended for experienced reefkeepers.
13.coloracao: Extremely varied: blue, purple, green, pink, cream

14.descricao: The second most popular SPS genus. Some species form plates, others form branches. Generally more forgiving than Acropora, making it a good gateway into the world of SPS corals.

15.descricao: Forms dense branches with rounded tips. One of the hardiest SPS corals, recommended as a first SPS for hobbyists transitioning to small polyp stony corals. Grows well and forms beautiful colonies.

16.descricao: Branching coral with a cauliflower-like appearance. One of the fastest growing and easiest SPS corals to keep. Frequently hosts small crabs that help clean debris.

17.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis, krill, and shrimp pieces
17.compatibilidade: Can move and sting neighboring corals
17.descricao: The most popular anemone in the marine aquarium hobby and the most readily accepted by captive-bred clownfish. Tentacles with bubble-shaped tips (under ideal conditions). Can reproduce through asexual division in the aquarium.

18.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts fish and shrimp pieces
18.compatibilidade: Highly stinging, can capture small fish
18.descricao: Large anemone with short, dense tentacles forming a carpet-like surface. Highly stinging - can capture and consume unwary fish. Requires sandy substrate for anchoring. For experienced reefkeepers only.

19.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis, krill, and shrimp pieces
19.descricao: One of the most beautiful and sought-after anemones, but also one of the most difficult to keep. Can reach over 50 cm in diameter. Natural host for several clownfish species. Demands pristine water parameters and intense lighting.
19.coloracao: Purple, blue, green with orange or white base

20.descricao: One of the easiest and hardiest corals. Bright green mat that spreads over rocks. Ideal for reef beginners.
20.coloracao: Fluorescent green on a purple base

21.compatibilidade: Peaceful, can detach and colonize new areas
21.descricao: Tree-shaped coral that reproduces easily by dropping fragments. Very hardy.

22.descricao: Wall variety of the Hammer Coral with a different growth form. Hammer-shaped tentacles with mesmerizing movement.

23.alimentacao: Photosynthetic, accepts mysis and brine shrimp
23.descricao: Easy to maintain LPS with large, attractive polyps. Actively feeds when food is offered.

24.descricao: Highly valued gold variety. Golden tentacles that glow under actinic lighting.

25.descricao: Pulsing variety of Xenia with constant rhythmic movement. Mesmerizing visual effect.

26.descricao: Large variety of the leather coral. Can reach 30cm+ in diameter.

27.descricao: Star-shaped polyps. Forms mats over rocks.

29.descricao: Leather coral with a lobed surface. Very hardy.

30.descricao: LPS with large, fleshy polyps. Great for beginners.

31.descricao: Branching LPS with round polyps at the tips.

32.descricao: Solitary LPS with spectacular colors. Lives on the substrate.

33.descricao: Highly collectible. Rare varieties are worth thousands.

34.descricao: SPS with thin, needle-like branches. Fast growth.

35.descricao: Encrusting SPS that forms plates. Relatively tolerant.

36.descricao: Very long and thin tentacles. Buries its foot in the substrate.

37.descricao: Caribbean anemone. Easy but rarely accepts clownfish.
